qa_pairs.question: What are the main reasons and changes users should be aware of when migrating from Singularity to Apptainer on Alliance systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Users are strongly recommended to use Apptainer instead of Singularity, as Apptainer is the renamed SingularityCE (up to v3.9.5). Key changes include added support for DMTCP checkpointing, removal of the `--nvccli` command line option and `apptainer build --remote`, replacement of the SylabsCloud remote endpoint with a DefaultRemote endpoint (though the previous default can be restored), and renaming all executables, paths, and environment variables from `singularity` to `apptainer` (e.g., `singularity` command becomes `apptainer`, `~/.singularity` directory becomes `~/.apptainer`, and `SINGULARITY` environment variables become `APPTAINER`). Apptainer version 1 is backwards compatible with Singularity, allowing for incremental script porting.

qa_pairs.question: Why is Docker not available on Alliance HPC clusters, and how can users still create Apptainer images from Dockerfiles?
qa_pairs.answer: Docker is not made available on Alliance HPC clusters because using it on a multiuser cluster creates security risks. However, users can install Docker on their own computer to create an Apptainer image, which can then be uploaded to an HPC cluster. Alternatively, it is often possible to build an Apptainer image directly from a Docker image.

qa_pairs.question: Why is the ability to use `sudo` for superuser/root permissions not available to users on Alliance clusters, and what alternatives are suggested for tasks that require it?
qa_pairs.answer: The ability to use `sudo` for superuser/root permissions is not available to users on Alliance clusters. For tasks requiring `sudo`, users can install Linux, Apptainer, and `sudo` in a virtual machine on a system they control to build images, then upload them to Alliance systems. Another option is to submit a technical support ticket to inquire if Alliance staff can assist in building the required images. Additionally, Apptainer version 1.1.x and newer offers improved support for `--fakeroot`, which may allow some images to be built from definition files or without needing `sudo`, although not all images will be buildable this way.
